questions.question: Which Ministry has launched the field assessment of 6th Swachh Survekshan?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

answer.label: C
answer.text: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has launched the field assessment for 6th edition of the Swachh Survekshan 2021.The Swachh Survekshan commenced in the year 2016 as a competition enhancing framework to encourage cities to achieve high standards of sanitation under the Swatch Bharat Mission.

questions.question: What is the IUCN status of Olive Ridley turtle?
questions.explanation: The mass nesting of the Olive Ridley turtles began in Puducherry coastline. The traditional migration season of the turtles delayed due to climate change or change in the climatic conditions because of the influence of Cyclones Nivar and Burevi.The turtles have been classified as vulnerable in the International Union for Conservation of Nature and Natural Resources (IUCN).

questions.question: Which Indian state recently approved Bill to reserve 75% private jobs for locals?
questions.explanation: The Governor of Haryana, Satyadev Narayan Arya, has approved a bill to reserve 75 per cent jobs in Private sector jobs for the people of the state. Reservation will be provided in every company, trust and society. Thus, the “Haryana State Employment of Local Candidates Act, 2020” has now come into force in the state of Haryana from March 2, 2020.

questions.question: PRASAD Scheme, which is in news recently, was launched under which Union ministry?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Tourism launched the PRASAD Scheme in the year 2014-2015. The full form of PRASAD Scheme is Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spirituality Augmentation Drive. The scheme aims at infrastructure development such as last mile connectivity, entry points, eco-friendly modes of transport, interpretation centres, ATM/Money exchanges, and other basic tourism facilities.

questions.question: Which rare species of native Australian bee was recently spotted along the east coast of Australia?
questions.explanation: Pharohylaeus lactiferous is a rare species of native Australian bee that was recently spotted along the east coast of Australia. It was last spotted a century ago in 1923 in Queensland and only 6 individuals have ever been found.The invertebrate is threatened by wildfires, land fragmentation and climate change.

questions.question: Which panel has recently been appointed by the Rajya Sabha Chairman regarding the two channels of the Parliament- RSTV and LSTV?
questions.explanation: Surya Prakash Committee is a panel appointed by the Rajya Sabha Chairman in consultation with the Lok Sabha Speaker to work out modalities regarding the two channels of the Parliament- RSTV and LSTV.The committee had suggested that the Parliament have a single channel by merging the two. It also suggested the use of 2 platforms for broadcast in Hindi and English.

questions.question: What is ‘APT41’, that was seen in news recently?
questions.explanation: APT41 (advanced persistent threat) is a cyber threat group that is known to carry out Chinese state sponsored cyber-attacks and other financially motivated cyber- attacks.It is also known as the Double Dragon. Since 2012, the group has attacked organisations in some 14 countries to steal intellectual properties (cyber espionage activities).

questions.question: What is the name of the unique type of specimen, which serves as the representation for the given species name?
questions.explanation: The term ‘holotype’ refers to an illustration or physical example of a species. It may also refer to one of several physical examples of the organism.It is a unique type specimen that is designated by the original author and serves as the representation for the given species name.

questions.question: Which state is to conduct its first survey of the social and educational conditions of people from backward classes?
questions.explanation: The Odisha government is set to begin its first state survey of the social and educational conditions of people from backward classes.In the state there are nearly 209 communities identified as socially and educationally backward classes (SEBCs). The classes comprise around 54 per cent of the state’s population. A proposal by the Odisha State Commission for Backward Classes (OSCBC) was approved in this regard.

questions.question: UNGA has declared 2023 as which special year, based on the resolution led by India?
questions.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly has unanimously declared 2023 as the International Year of Millets. The unanimously adopted resolution was sponsored by India and supported by over 70 nations. The co-sponsors of the declaration include Bangladesh, Kenya, Nepal, Nigeria, Russia and Senegal, and all Member States of the UN.
